## What does this PR do?

<!-- 1–3 sentences. What changes and why. -->

## Type of change

<!-- Check one -->

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that causes existing behavior to change)
- [ ] Documentation / tooling only
- [ ] Chore (CI, deps, formatting — no functional change)

## Scope check

<!-- Confirm the PR fits endpulse's narrow niche -->

- [ ] This is a health-check feature, not load testing
- [ ] This does not require a persistent server or web UI
- [ ] If a new flag/key is added, it is documented in README + CHANGELOG

## Test plan

<!-- List the tests added or the commands you ran manually -->

- [ ] `pytest -v` passes locally
- [ ] `ruff check endpulse/ tests/` passes
- [ ] `mypy endpulse/` passes
- [ ] Manual: <describe what you ran>

## Related issue / discussion

<!-- Link issue or discussion if any: Closes #… / Relates to #… -->

## Checklist

- [ ] I read the [Code of Conduct](.github/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
- [ ] I signed off commits (if required)
- [ ] I updated `README.md` for user-facing changes
- [ ] I updated `CHANGELOG.md` under `## [Unreleased]`
- [ ] I did not add runtime dependencies without discussing first
